reports.text:
POEL-PGPL Merger Approved (MCap ₹585 Cr)

Merger Key Points  
- POEL absorbs PGPL (effective Apr 1, 2026)  
- Needs BSE/SEBI/NCLT/shareholder approvals  
- PGPL: -₹11.5 Cr net worth, ₹139 Cr turnover  

Swap Ratio  
- 13 POEL shares/100 PGPL equity  
- 5 POEL shares/100 PGPL pref shares  

Strategic Benefits  
- Vertical integration (lead recycling)  
- Western India expansion  
- Scale economies  

Financials  
- POEL net worth ₹182 Cr  
- Public shareholding to 60.59%  

Key Takeaway  
Synergy play at cheap 585 Cr valuation

Cupid Ltd Boosts Condom Capacity with Dual-Polymer Facility (MCap ₹11,080 Cr)

Facility Highlights  
- New plant for NRL + Nitrile male/female condoms  
- Capacity: 1.25 Bn male + 125 Mn female annually  

Market Opportunity  
- Global female condom mkt: USD 1.2 Bn by 2030 (from USD 770 Mn 2024)  
- Nitrile premiums: 25-35% over latex  

Strategic Edge  
- Addresses UNFPA/ministries diversification needs  
- NRL female condom: WHO/UNFPA prequalified, 50+ countries  

Key Takeaway  
Nitrile entry unlocks premium global volumes at 11k Cr mcap

NOCIL Greenlights ₹130 Cr Rubber Chemicals Expansion (MCap ₹2,416 Cr)

Expansion Details  
- ₹130 Cr capex for Dahej plant  
- Targets rubber chemicals + intermediates  
- Current: 115k MTA at 70% utilization  
- Brownfield with backward integration  

Timeline & Funding  
- Completion: H1 FY28  
- Financed via internal accruals  

Strategic Aims  
- Revenue growth boost  
- Higher efficiency  
- Better market positioning  

Key Takeaway  
Capacity expansion de-risks growth at sub-2.5k Cr mcap

Fiem Industries Commissions EMI/EMC Lab (MCap ₹5,726 Cr)

Lab Highlights  
- New EMI/EMC facility in Gurugram  
- Tests 2W/4W lighting electronics  
- In-house regulatory/OEM compliance  

Key Benefits  
- Faster development cycles  
- Lower external testing costs  
- Higher OEM confidence  

Key Takeaway  
Tech upgrade bolsters auto lighting edge at 5.7k Cr mcap

Fedfina AUM Crosses ₹17,500 Cr Milestone (MCap ₹4,893 Cr)

AUM Growth  
- Hit ₹17,500 Cr as of Dec 2025, reflecting strong expansion.  

Profitability Turnaround  
- RoMA rebounds to 1.8% in 9MFY26 after FY25 stress in unsecured loans.  

Balance Sheet Strength  
- Gearing steady at 4.0x; CRAR robust at 20.5% (Dec 2025).  
- Liquidity solid: ₹1,067 Cr cash/bank balances + ₹1,937 Cr unavailed lines (Jan 2026).  

Key Takeaway  
AUM scale + improving RoMA signals earnings recovery—watch at sub-5k Cr mcap.

🗞 BEAT THE STREET BULLETIN 🗞
Date: 16 March 2026
Telegram: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

📍 ORDER WIN

POWER MECH PROJECTS: Wins ₹709.56 crore O&M contract from Adani Infrastructure for Tiroda thermal power plant operations.

CEIGALL INDIA: JV with RIPL emerges L1 bidder for ₹274.08 crore EPC highway project (NH-913 Frontier Highway) in Arunachal Pradesh.

RVNL: Receives ₹95.27 crore order from NMDC for refurbishment of railway tracks and sidings in Chhattisgarh.

DANISH POWER: Secures ₹48.77 crore orders from EPC and IPP companies for inverter duty transformers supply.

RAILTEL: Bags ₹42.63 crore NICSI order for New Core Link under National Knowledge Network project.

MAHINDRA EPC IRRIGATION: Wins ₹17.95 crore contract to supply micro irrigation systems covering ~3,591 hectares.

MARINE ELECTRICALS: Receives ₹17.12 crore order from Princeton Digital Group for power distribution systems.

GREENLEAF ENVIROTECH: Receives multiple work orders worth ₹14.62 crore for STP installation, upgrades and maintenance.

GUJARAT INJECT (KERALA): Receives ₹6.07 crore order from Earthwave Technology for supply of 7,041 solar PV modules.

GABION TECHNOLOGIES: Receives multiple domestic supply orders worth ₹2.45 crore for gabion boxes and geotextile products.

TEJAS NETWORKS: Selected for 4G network expansion project in South Asia to supply advanced RAN solutions.

ASIAN ENERGY SERVICES: Vedanta extends O&M contract for Cambay oil & gas processing facility till March 2027.

---

📍 ACQUISITION

BHARAT FORGE: PI Opportunities Fund invests ₹300 crore in subsidiary JS Auto for 23% stake via equity and CCPS.

NIDHI GRANITES: Step-down subsidiary SPNP Paper to sell 100% stake in Fine Papyrus to NGL Corporation for ₹3.24 crore.

---

📍 COLLABORATION

BARTRONICS INDIA: Avio Smart Market Stack signs MoU with AgroSperity to build digital agriculture ecosystem across 5,000+ villages.

GMDC: Signs MoU with NMDC to explore collaboration in rare earth elements exploration and development.

IZMO: Subsidiary Izmo Microsystems partners with CCRRAFT and Alcyon Photonics to develop silicon photonics solutions.

INTELLECT DESIGN ARENA: Forms 50:50 JV with Fintel PLC in the UK to develop AI-led financial advisory platform.

---

📍 LEADERSHIP CHANGES

BAJAJ ELECTRICALS: Appoints Suketu Shah as Interim CFO effective March 16, 2026.

UPDATER SERVICES: Appoints Ram Praveen Radhakrishnan as Group CFO effective March 17, 2026.

SIGMA ADVANCED SYSTEMS: Reconstitutes board, adding defence and diplomatic experts including Lt Gen B.S. Raju (Retd.).

---

📍 NEW PROJECTS

RNIT AI SOLUTIONS: Signs agreement with Andhra Pradesh government to deploy AI facial-recognition attendance system across ~3,900 institutions.

SUBA HOTELS: Signs franchise term sheet to develop 46-room Comfort Inn hotel in Orai, Uttar Pradesh.

---

📍 FUNDRAISING

THERMAX: Subsidiary First Energy signs agreement with India Cements for equity investment giving ~14.63% stake.

---

📍 CREDIT RATINGS

PAISALO DIGITAL: Receives ‘BWR AA / Stable’ rating from Brickwork for proposed ₹1,500 crore NCD issuance.

---

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

ALLCARGO TERMINALS: February volumes rise 8% YoY to 57.6k TEUs.

---

📍 USFDA / REGULATORY

SUN PHARMA: US FDA accepts sBLA for ILUMYA to treat psoriatic arthritis; decision expected by Oct 29, 2026.

---

📍 CLARIFICATION

FINO PAYMENTS BANK: Says media reports on PMLA probe expansion are speculative and unrelated to bank GST compliance.

IDBI BANK: Clarifies it cannot confirm reports of scrapping strategic disinvestment as process handled by Government of India.

BANDHAN BANK: Denies awareness of promoter stake sale discussions cited in media reports.

---

📍 LAUNCHES

GLENMARK PHARMA: Launches Potassium Phosphates Injection USP in the US institutional injectable market.

KUNDAN EDIFICE: Develops flexible wall washer lighting solution for curved architectural surfaces.

LTTS: Launches NVIDIA-powered AI Lung Digital Twin platform for advanced respiratory diagnostics.

---

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

SUNNITA TOOLS: Incorporates US subsidiary Sunita Defence Inc. to expand defence business.

CRYOGENIC OGS: Incorporates wholly owned subsidiary Cryogenic OGS Middle East F.Z.E. in UAE.

AVI POLYMERS: Launches AI healthcare subsidiary AVI AI Technologies Pvt Ltd.

UCAL: Dilutes stake in UCAL Holdings after AscentX Inc acquires 90% stake.

---

📍 TAX ORDER

DODLA DAIRY: Subsidiary HR Food Processing receives ₹18.11 crore income tax demand for AY 2024-25.

---

📍 ADDITIONS

EXICOM TELE-SYSTEMS: Commences commercial production at new Telangana plant for EV chargers and battery systems.

LLOYDS METALS & ENERGY: Begins commercial production of copper cathodes from 12,000 TPA plant in Congo.

INDIAN METALS & FERRO ALLOYS: Starts operations at two additional furnaces at Kalinganagar ferro chrome plant.

FIEM INDUSTRIES: Commissions EMI/EMC testing laboratory in Gurugram for automotive electronics validation.

---

📍 OTHERS

GOODLUCK INDIA: Dispatches first overseas shipment of 155 mm defence shells worth USD 6 million.

POLYCAB INDIA: Flags weak outlook with flat Q4 volumes and margin pressure due to export softness.

CITY UNION BANK: Opens two new branches in Tamil Nadu and Madhya Pradesh, taking total network to 927.

TATA MOTORS: To raise commercial vehicle prices by up to 1.5% from April 1 to offset input cost pressures.

NOCIL: Approves ₹130 crore capex to expand rubber chemicals capacity at Dahej plant.

ACCEDERE: Positions itself as AI-driven cyber compliance company with drone-based analytics platforms.

CUPID: Begins development of nitrile female condom targeting global premium market.

CUPID: Launches “Made in India with Japanese Quality” branding strategy for global positioning.

---

New Projects  
- Omaxe World Mall: Faridabad (HRERA to Nov 2030).  
- The Retreat: Amritsar (Punjab RERA to Dec 2031).

Recent Wins  
- Mar 12: 4 Mohali residential RERA nods.  
- Mar 6: Mohali Urban Square-14 commercial.  
- Mar 5: Mohali Lake Commercial Ph2.  
- Feb 18: 2 Faridabad projects.  
- Broader: Rs 500 Cr Ludhiana; Rs 1,000 Cr Amritsar township.  

Key Takeaway  
RERA unlocks sales in key North India markets—pipeline value at low mcap demands attention.

Omaxe ; Subsidiaries Receive RERA Approval for Two Projects

• 🏢 Regulatory Approval: Omaxe Limited subsidiaries receive RERA registration certificates for two projects.

• 🏬 Project 1: Omaxe World Mall, Faridabad (Commercial mix-use project).

• 🏗️ Project 2: The Retreat, Amritsar (Commercial built-up project).

• 📅 Launch Date: Both projects scheduled for 17 March 2026 launch.

• 📜 Validity: RERA approvals valid till 2030–2031 unless extended.

🟢 Impact: Positive
RERA approvals enable project launch and marketing, improving sales visibility and supporting Omaxe’s real estate development pipeline.

reports.text:
India–Iran Talks Over Tankers Amid Hormuz Passage Concerns - REUTERS 

• ⚓ Tanker Issue: Iran asks India to release three tankers seized in February.

• 🌊 Hormuz Safety: Discussions aimed at ensuring safe passage for Indian-flagged or India-bound ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

• 🛢️ Energy Trade Risk: Any disruption in Hormuz could impact global crude shipments and oil supply chains.

• 🤝 Diplomatic Talks: Iranian ambassador held talks with Indian foreign ministry officials to resolve the issue.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slight Negative
Geopolitical tension around the Strait of Hormuz raises uncertainty for global oil trade and could increase crude price volatility.

Thermax ; India Cements Invests in First Energy Pvt Ltd

• 🤝 Strategic Investment: Thermax Limited subsidiary First Energy Pvt Ltd (FEPL) signs share subscription agreement with India Cements Limited.

• 💰 Equity Infusion: India Cements to subscribe 1.33 crore shares at ₹10 each + ₹4.12 premium.

• 📊 Stake Structure: Post investment, India Cements to hold ~14.63% stake in FEPL.

• 📉 Ownership Change: Thermax shareholding in FEPL reduces to ~62.15%.

• ⚡ Business Link: FEPL provides clean energy / biofuel solutions for industrial users.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slight Positive
Strategic partnership with India Cements strengthens FEPL’s customer linkage and funding base, though Thermax’s stake dilution slightly reduces ownership.

Paisalo Digital ; Gets ‘BWR AA / Stable’ Rating for ₹1,500 Cr NCD Issue

• 🏦 Credit Rating Upgrade: Paisalo Digital Limited receives ‘BWR AA / Stable’ rating from Brickwork Ratings for proposed NCDs.

• 💰 Fundraising Plan: Rating assigned for ₹1,500 crore Non-Convertible Debenture (NCD) issuance.

• 📊 Dual Rating: Complements existing credit rating from Infomerics Analytics, strengthening credit profile.

• 📈 Strong Fundamentals: Rating supported by scalable lending model, strong capitalization, and stable asset quality.

• 🤝 Investor Access: Dual rating expands access to wider debt investors and capital markets.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Strong credit rating enhances borrowing ability, improves investor confidence, and supports Paisalo’s lending growth strategy.

Entry into AI-driven healthcare opens new growth avenues and positions AVI Polymers in the rapidly expanding digital health technology segment.

reports.text:
NOCIL ; Approves ₹130 Cr Capex for Dahej Plant Expansion

• 🏭 Capacity Expansion: NOCIL Limited approves ₹130 crore capex for expanding rubber chemicals capacity at Dahej plant.

• 📊 Existing Capacity: Current capacity stands at 115,000 MTA, with ~70% utilization.

• ⚙️ Integrated Facility: Expansion includes backward integration of key inputs and specialty rubber chemicals production.

• 💰 Funding Plan: Investment to be funded largely through internal accruals.

• ⏳ Timeline: Project targeted to be completed by H1 FY28.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Capacity expansion with backward integration can improve operational efficiency, strengthen market share, and support long-term growth in the rubber chemicals segment.

Intellect Design Arena ; Forms AI-Focused JV with Fintel in UK

• 🤝 Strategic JV: Intellect Design Arena Limited partners with Fintel PLC to form a 50:50 joint venture in the UK.

• 🤖 AI-First Platform: JV will deploy Intellect’s AI-driven Agentic platform to transform the UK financial advisory ecosystem.

• 💼 Target Market: Focus on wealth management and financial advisory firms in the UK market.

• ⚙️ Technology Integration: Platform integrates AI workflow orchestration, compliance monitoring, and advisory lifecycle management.

• 🌍 Global Expansion: Partnership strengthens Intellect’s presence in the UK fintech and wealth advisory technology space.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Strategic JV expands Intellect’s global footprint and strengthens positioning in AI-driven financial technology solutions.

reports.text:
Dodla Dairy ; Subsidiary Receives ₹18.11 Cr Income Tax Demand

• 📄 Tax Order Issued: Dodla Dairy Limited subsidiary HR Food Processing Pvt Ltd receives assessment order under Section 143(3) of the Income Tax Act.

• 🏦 Tax Demand: Authorities raised ₹18.11 crore demand for AY 2024–25.

• ⚖️ Company Disputes Order: Company states adjustments are not in line with the merits of the case.

• 🧾 Appeal Planned: Subsidiary is filing an appeal before the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals).

• 📊 Management View: Company believes tax demand is not sustainable.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slight Negative
Tax demand creates a short-term overhang, but impact may remain limited if the company succeeds in appeal.

reports.text:
FIEM Industries ; Commissions New EMI/EMC Testing Laboratory

• 🏭 New Facility: FIEM Industries Limited inaugurates state-of-the-art EMI/EMC testing laboratory at Gurugram.

• 💡 Automotive Focus: Facility to support automotive lighting electronics for two-wheelers and four-wheelers.

• 🔬 In-House Testing: Enables EMI/EMC validation tests internally, ensuring compliance with OEM and regulatory standards.

• ⚙️ Faster Product Development: Helps accelerate product development and reduce reliance on external testing facilities.

• 📈 Technology Strengthening: Enhances engineering capabilities and backward integration in validation infrastructure.

🟢 Impact: Positive
New testing facility strengthens FIEM’s R&D capabilities, improves product development efficiency, and supports growth in advanced automotive lighting solutions.

• 📜 Major Order Win: Power Mech Projects Limited secures contract from Adani Infrastructure Management Services Limited.

• ⚡ Project Scope: Operations & Maintenance (O&M) services including overhauling at 5×660 MW thermal power plant.

• 📍 Project Location: Tiroda Thermal Power Plant, Maharashtra.

• 💰 Contract Value: Order worth ₹709.56 crore (excluding GST).

• ⏳ Long-Term Contract: Execution period of 60 months (Apr 2026 – Mar 2031).

🟢 Impact: Positive
Large long-term O&M contract strengthens Power Mech’s order book and ensures steady revenue visibility.

Greenleaf Envirotech ; Receives ₹14.62 Cr Work Orders

• 📜 Order Wins: Greenleaf Envirotech Limited receives multiple work orders worth ₹14.62 crore.

• 🏭 STP Projects: Orders include sewage treatment plant (STP) installation and upgrade projects in Madhya Pradesh and Karnataka.

• 🛠️ Industrial Maintenance: Contract from Hindalco Industries Limited for ETP plant operation & maintenance in Silvassa.

• 🤝 Key Clients: Projects awarded by Helios Infra, Hindalco Industries, and Oasis EPC Solutions.

• 📊 Project Scope: Includes STP installation, EMI supply, plant upgrades, and environmental infrastructure services.

Cryogenic OGS ; Incorporates Wholly-Owned Subsidiary in UAE

• 🌍 International Expansion: Cryogenic OGS Limited incorporates wholly-owned subsidiary “Cryogenic OGS Middle East F.Z.E.” in the UAE.

• 💰 Initial Capital: Investment of AED 1,00,000 for 100% shareholding.

• 🏭 Business Focus: Subsidiary to engage in heavy equipment & machinery spare parts trading and import-export activities.

• 📈 Growth Strategy: Move aimed at exploring business opportunities and expanding presence in Middle East markets.

• 🏢 Free Zone Entity: Company incorporated under Ajman Free Zones Authority (UAE) regulations.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Overseas subsidiary strengthens Cryogenic OGS’s global footprint and may support growth in the Middle East oil & gas equipment market.

Tata Motors CV ; Announces Price Hike for Commercial Vehicles

• 🚚 Price Increase: Tata Motors Limited to raise commercial vehicle prices by up to 1.5%.

Indian Metals & Ferro Alloys Ltd — Scaling Ferrochrome & Mining Expansion

MCap: ₹6,814.9 Cr 
Industry: Mining & Minerals

Production Targets
- FY27 ferrochrome target: 4,00,000 tonnes
- FY28 ferrochrome target: 4,75,000–5,00,000 tonnes
- Ore raising scaling to 1 million tonnes by FY27

Capacity Expansion — KNR
- KNR1 furnace 1: commissioned June 2026
- KNR1 furnace 2: commissioned September 2026
- Tata Kalinganagar (KNR2) acquisition closing Q4 FY26
- KNR2 volumes contributing from Q1 FY27

Cost Advantages
- EBITDA cost to fall ₹1,500–2,000/tonne on logistics gains
- Fully captive ore supply — zero external ore purchases
- 110 MW hybrid renewable PPAs signed at ₹3.75–3.84/unit from June 2026

Sales Mix Shift
- Current: 90% export / 10% domestic
- Target: 60% export / 40% domestic over 2 years

Mining Upgrade
- Sukinda mine: 300 kt open-cast → 600 kt underground
- Underground mining capex: ₹1,000 Cr over 4–5 years

New Business — Ethanol
- 120 KLD ethanol plant commissioning March 2026
- Capex: ₹150 Cr | Contribution from FY27

Capital Allocation
- FY26 total capex: ₹800–900 Cr — funded via internal accruals
- Peak debt ceiling: ₹470 Cr
- Target D/E: ≤0.3x

Realization Outlook
- Domestic: ₹1,18,000–1,20,000/tonne
- Export: $0.96–0.97/lb
- South African supply constraints + chrome ore inflation = supportive pricing

Key Takeaway
- Volume nearly 2x by FY28 vs current levels
- Cost reduction + captive ore = strong margin expansion ahead
- Clean balance sheet with disciplined capex funding
- Renewable energy + domestic mix shift = ESG & margin tailwind

The Shrinking MOAT — A Risk Investors Are Underpricing

The Core Observation
- Sanjoy Bhattacharya: *"Period of competitive advantage has shortened dramatically worldwide"*
- MOATs that once lasted decades are now lasting years
- Rate of disruption is accelerating faster than ever

The Valuation Trap
- Assigning high terminal value to MOATs = hidden risk
- Markets price in perpetual competitive advantage
- When MOAT breaks → sudden & sharp de-rating event
- Multiples built on MOATs can become irrational until they collapse

Why MOATs Are Breaking Faster
- Democratisation of capital — funding is more accessible
- Technology — levels the playing field rapidly
- AI — disrupts conventional business models at scale
- Lower barriers — new entrants can replicate faster than before

30 Years Ago vs Today
- Earlier: MOATs were extremely difficult to break
- Today: Competitive advantages are far more vulnerable
- Conventional business models now face real terminal value risk

The Nuanced View
- Some MOATs do deserve premium multiples
- But assuming perpetuity is where the danger lies
- Investors must ask: *"Is this MOAT still intact — or just priced as if it is?"*

Key Takeaway
- Price in MOAT durability — not just MOAT existence
- A broken MOAT + high multiple = significant wealth destruction
- In today's world, re-examine every terminal value assumption

reports.text:
West Asia Tensions Weigh on India’s Gems & Jewellery Exports  

Export Pressure Mounts  
- India’s gems and jewellery exports have declined ~20%, impacted by geopolitical instability in West Asia, per GJEPC.  
- If disruptions persist, exports worth ~$1.2 billion could be hit in the next three months.  

Regional Dependence  
- About 20% of India’s jewellery exports are directed to West Asia, exposing the sector to regional volatility.  
- The UAE and GCC together account for ~$8.3 billion in exports, with Dubai serving as a major hub for polished diamond trading.  

Trade Linkages at Risk  
- Gold imports to the UAE (~200 tonnes annually) could face hurdles, given the India–UAE CEPA dependencies in the supply chain.  

Broader Context  
- From Apr 2025–Feb 2026, exports were flat at $25.93 billion, nearly unchanged from $25.92 billion YoY, reflecting a sluggish global demand cycle.  
- The industry is also navigating global trade uncertainties and potential US tariff pressures, further straining export momentum.  

Key Takeaway:  
Persistent West Asia tensions and global trade headwinds could delay recovery for India’s jewellery export sector, underscoring the need for market diversification and supply-chain resilience.

**Home Decor Sector — Raw Material Shock | Nuvama Sector Update Mar-26**

**The Big Picture**
- **US–Iran conflict** disrupting Middle East energy & chemicals
- Near-term **margin pressure** across pipes, tiles & wood panels
- Broad-based **price hikes now inevitable**

**PVC & Plastic Pipes**
- PVC resin up **47% since Jan-26** → **₹101/kg**
- Crude up **43%** to **$86.7/bbl**
- Some manufacturers **stopped taking orders** due to price volatility
- **Q4FY26** expected to be strongest quarter on volumes & EBITDA/tonne
- Inventory losses of **9MFY26 reversing into gains**

**Jal Jeevan Mission — Residual Demand**
- JJM outlay: **₹8.7 Tn** | Rural tap coverage: **17% → 82%**
- High pending states: **Rajasthan (41%)**, **WB (43%)**, **Jharkhand (45%)**, **Kerala (45%)**, **MP (26%)**
- Residual JJM work offers **further demand upside** for pipe players

**Ceramics — Severe Stress**
- Gujarat Gas cut Morbi supply to **50%** from March 6
- **~100 Morbi units** already shut
- Up to **70% factories** could shut if propane stops
- **~1,500 export containers** stuck due to Hormuz tensions
- War-risk surcharge: **$4,000–5,000/container** vs normal **$300–600**
- Profit/container **~$300** — margins completely **wiped out**

**Wood Panels — Cost Inflation**
- Methanol **+46%** | Formaldehyde **+35%** | Melamine **+25%**
- Technical Urea **+24%** | Wax **+13%**
- MDF cost inflation: **7–10%** depending on grade
- Particle board RMs up **40–60%**
- Price hikes of **5–10%** already guided by smaller players

**Key Takeaway**
- **Pipes:** Near-term positive — volume & inventory gains in Q4FY26
- **Ceramics:** High risk — export margins destroyed, factory shutdowns likely
- **Wood Panels:** Price hikes inevitable — margin protection key monitorable
- Current PVC spike seen as **unsustainable** — correction likely ahead

Demand Spike  
- Instant foods, ready-to-cook meals, and frozen snacks sales up ~20% amid LPG cylinder shortages.  
- Consumers opting for ready meals, frozen veggies, noodles, and quick kits to bypass cooking gas dependency.  

Retail Response  
- Retailers and quick-commerce platforms ramping up ready-meal inventories to capitalize on the demand wave.  

Alternative Solutions  
- Households snapping up induction cooktops and electric appliances as LPG uncertainty persists.  

Market Outlook  
- Trend may drive permanent shift to convenience foods, particularly among urban working professionals.  
- Ready-to-cook meal market at $7.1 billion (2025), projected to reach $12 billion by 2034.  

Key Takeaway:  
LPG supply disruptions are accelerating adoption of convenience foods, creating sustained growth tailwinds for the $12B ready-to-eat market by 2034.

US Gold Cover: Debt Reality Check  

US gold reserves (8,133.5 tons, world's largest) now back just 3% of federal debt**—near record low despite gold's all-time highs.  

**Historical Shift  
- 1980: ~18% coverage (6x current). Matching requires gold at $26,000/oz (+400%).  
- 1940s: >50% backing. Parity demands $75,000/oz (+1,340%).  

Debt Explosion  
Reserves static while debt balloons—exposes fiscal imbalance gold bugs have flagged for years.  

Market Signal  
Low ratio fuels gold as hedge narrative, especially with Trump-era deficits. Inflation/BRICS de-dollar talk adds tailwinds. Relevant for your commodity watch (Gravita exposure)?

JSW Steel to Develop Coking Coal Mine in Mozambique

JSW Steel announced plans to develop a coking coal mining project in Mozambique to secure long-term raw material supply for its steel operations.

The project, Minas de Revuboè (MdR), is located in the Moatize coal basin in Tete Province.

The mine holds ~850 million tonnes of coal reserves, with potential to produce about 250 million tonnes of usable coking coal.

JSW plans to develop the mine in phases, with the first phase expected to be completed in about 2.5 years.

Initial production target is 2.4 million tonnes per year of prime hard coking coal.

reports.text:
Multiple De-Rating — The Case of Hindustan Foods

The Core Problem
- Stock remains largely flat over last 5 years
- Despite strong financial performance by the company
- Root cause: Extremely high starting valuation

Valuation Journey
- 2021: Stock trading at 150x+ P/E
- Today: Multiples compressed to ~40x
- Sharp de-rating of 110x+ turns over 4 years

Where It Stands Now
- Management guided PAT of ₹200–220 Cr for FY27
- Stock currently trading at ~25–26x FY27E earnings
- Valuation has significantly normalised

The Irony
- Business execution has remained consistently strong
- Yet stock has delivered near-zero returns
- Investors who paid 150x are still sitting on losses

The Key Lesson
- Entry valuation matters more than business quality
- Even great businesses can be bad investments at wrong prices
- De-rating can silently erode returns for years

Broader Pattern
- This is not an isolated case
- Similar de-rating has played out across multiple stocks
- High P/E entry = long years of time correction ahead

- World's #1 defence importer → now a global manufacturing hub

Budget & Scale
- Defence budget: ₹7.85L Cr — 4th largest globally
- Domestic procurement driving self-reliance at scale

Live Programs
- 97 Tejas Mk-1A — ₹**62,000 Cr** order
- 156 Prachand Helicopters — ₹**62,700 Cr** order
- Tata-Airbus manufacturing jets in Vadodara

Export Surge

- Growth of 35x in a single decade
- Target: ₹**50,000 Cr** by FY29

Space Economy
- 300+ startups active in the sector
- ₹**10,000 Cr** dedicated fund deployed
- India's share: 2% → 8% of global space economy by 2033
- Total opportunity: $44 Billion+

The Journey
- Importer → Exporter → Global Hub

Hatsun Agro: Founder’s FY26–27 Roadmap  

Revenue Outlook  
- FY26 run-rate: ~₹10,000 Cr (±0.5%).  
- FY27 growth: Positive, hinges on easing global conflicts impacting packaging supply.  
- Milk remains core driver across all product categories.  

Pricing Dynamics  
- Milk procurement up 8–10% (fat shortage from exports).  
- Selling prices hiked ~6% to partially offset costs.  

Consumer Focus  
- Prioritizing consumer segment over HoReCa.  
- Zero credit policy + home consumption to gain in uncertain conditions.  

Acquisition Progress  
- Recent business fully integrated; standalone reporting by Q3 FY26 end.  
- Early challenges resolved; volumes improving.  

Supply Chain Pressures  
- Plastic packaging costs up 30–40% due to global conflicts.  
- Gas shortages hit wafer cone production.  
- In-house polythene film offers partial hedge; 1–1.5 months inventory available.  

Regulatory Boost  
- FSSAI scrutiny on unorganized dairy likely to favor organized players like Hatsun.  

Geographic Expansion  
- 45% revenue now from outside Tamil Nadu; targeting 50% in 2 years.  
- New markets to drive higher capacity utilization.  

Financial Edge  
- Gross margins 1.5–2× peers via 4,500 owned outlets (no wholesalers).  
- Depreciation to decline post heavy infra capex.  
- Debt-equity <1:1 (improved from 1:2–1:3).  

Key Takeaway:  
Hatsun’s direct model superiority, geographic push, and regulatory tailwinds position it to capture ₹10,000 Cr revenue milestone and margin outperformance despite global headwinds.

PhonePe Pauses IPO Plans Amid Global Uncertainty

What Happened?
• PhonePe has temporarily halted its IPO process
• Decision driven by ongoing geopolitical conflicts & market volatility
• CEO Sameer Nigam confirmed the pause officially

Investor Roadshow — Mixed Signals
• PhonePe recently conducted global investor roadshows
• Received mixed responses from international investors
• Global investors prefer to wait out current geopolitical situation

Valuation Concern
• PhonePe's targeted valuation of $10 Billion seen as too high in current market conditions
• Investors not comfortable committing at peak valuations amid uncertainty

What's Next?
• PhonePe will resume IPO process once global markets stabilise
• Company remains committed to listing in India
• CEO stated: *"We sincerely hope for a swift return to peace in all affected regions"*

Paytm Angle — Positive
• Paytm (+VE) — PhonePe's IPO delay removes a near-term competitive overhang
• One less large fintech listing competing for investor capital in the near term
• Positive sentiment for Paytm in the domestic digital payments space

**Jefferies BUY Divi’s Labs: TP ₹8,100 (28% Upside)**  

**GLP/Peptide Growth Engine**  
- **Two oral GLP intermediates (150 MT ea.) + injectable peptide**: **$1.1B annual sales by FY32** ($630M oral, $426M injectable).  
- Drives **15% revenue CAGR, 20% PAT CAGR** (FY26–32, constant currency).  

**Sales/EBITDA Outlook**  
- Revenue to **$2.7B, EBITDA $1.1B by FY32**.  
- Margins expand: **33% (FY26E) → 41% (FY32)**; PAT **$822M** (~30% margin).  

**Capex Ramp**  
- **FY26 capex >₹20B**; CWIP at decade highs.  
- **25–30% incremental capacity** pending for GLP/peptide scale-up.  

**Guidance & Valuation**  
- **FY26–28**: Revenue **16% CAGR**, EBITDA **21% CAGR**, EPS **₹82.5 → ₹141.9**.  
- Trades at **compressing P/E** on earnings acceleration.  

**Offset Factors**  
- **Entresto decline ~$180M** by FY32; growth from **custom synthesis, peptides, contrast media, nutraceuticals, generics**.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Divi’s **GLP/peptide CDMO jackpot** fuels **high-margin re-rating**, with capex unlocking **$2.7B sales**—prime multi-year compounding play.

AI Boom Set to Power Next Global Infrastructure Cycle  

Industry Outlook  
- Redington Ltd predicts the AI workload surge will kickstart a new global infrastructure expansion wave.  
- The company is transforming from a tech distributor to an ecosystem orchestrator, emphasizing cloud, services, and value-added integration.  

Infrastructure Demand Spike  
- Accelerating AI adoption is fueling need for high-performance computing (HPC) — spanning GPUs, data centres, advanced networks, and large-scale storage systems.  

Strategic Partnerships  
- Redington is collaborating with cloud players, hyperscalers, OEMs, and GPU makers to deliver AI-ready infrastructure for enterprises across on-premise and private cloud setups.  

Enterprise Adoption Curve  
- Corporates are piloting AI cautiously, balancing ROI goals, data maturity, and workforce readiness before full-scale rollout.  

AI Within Redington  
- Internally, AI and analytics are driving better forecasting, supply chain decisions, and warehouse efficiency across 40+ global markets.  
- Deployment of AI copilots and automation tools is enhancing inventory management, procurement, and operations.  

Key Takeaway:  
Redington’s pivot toward AI-driven infrastructure orchestration positions it at the core of the next computing cycle, enabling enterprises to scale efficiently in the age of intelligent automation.

• 🏦 Exchange Clarification: IDBI Bank responds to exchange query regarding media report on possible scrapping of strategic stake sale.

• 📢 No Confirmation: Bank states it cannot confirm or deny the report as the disinvestment process is handled by the Government of India.

• 🏛️ Process Underway: Strategic disinvestment is being conducted through competitive bidding under GOI guidelines.

• 📩 No Official Communication: Bank says no communication received from GOI about cancelling the stake sale.

• 📊 No Business Impact: Bank states the news article has no material impact on operations or financials.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Clarification removes speculation, but disinvestment uncertainty remains until official government update.

Key Takeaway:  
India’s startup funding landscape remains soft, with capital selectively flowing to high-conviction, early-stage bets as investors reassess risk appetite in 2026.

Cold Chain Players Gear Up for Summer Quick-Commerce Surge  

Seasonal Prep Underway  
- Cold-chain logistics firms are expanding capacity and networks ahead of a hotter-than-normal summer and rising quick-commerce demand.  

Demand Spike  
- Platforms like Zepto, Blinkit, and Swiggy Instamart saw ~30% higher summer orders last year, led by ice creams, cold beverages, and other perishables.  
- Perishables make up ~40% of quick-commerce orders, underscoring the need for strong temperature-controlled networks.  

Operational Expansion  
- Logistics players are reinforcing hyperlocal micro-fulfilment hubs to ensure faster delivery and cold storage continuity.  
- Celsius Logistics has increased manpower and expanded distribution centers, readying for high-volume operations.  

Tech-Enabled Cold Solutions  
- Bobba Logistics is piloting pre-conditioned cooling vests, mini refrigerated backpacks, and flash-freezing chambers for more efficient last-mile delivery.  
- Enhanced visibility through QR-based tracking, temperature sensors, wearable alerts, and smart cooling systems helps preserve product integrity.  

Key Takeaway:  
With quick-commerce volumes heating up, cold-chain firms are investing in tech-driven, hyperlocal infrastructure to capture the summer demand boom and ensure delivery consistency at scale.
Advanced Mutual Funds terms

**Why SIPs Thrive in Crashes: 2008 Proof**  

**Peak vs. Dip Reality**  
- **Jan 2008 Peak (NAV ₹16.84)**: ₹10K SIP → **79K** (XIRR 12.8%), **593 units**.  

**Power of Falling NAVs**  
- NAV crashes to **₹12, 9, 7, 6, 5** → SIP buys **~1,000 units** (more shares at bargain prices).  

**Dip Installments Win Big**  
- **2009 Bottom SIP**: ₹10K → **₹2.8L** (₹2.7L gain vs. ₹69K at peak).  
- **Similar holding periods**, but **exponential unit accumulation** drives superior returns.  

**Strategy Edge**  
- **Continue SIPs**: Rupee cost averaging maximizes long-term compounding.  
- **Legends top up**; **ultra-legends lump sum** on deep dips.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Falling markets = **SIP superpower**—buy more units cheap, ride the rebound for outsized gains like 2008’s 28x bottom returns. Stay invested.

India’s Weight-Loss Drug Market Set for ₹5,000 Cr Surge by 2030  

Market Outlook  
- India’s weight-loss drug market may grow 5x to ₹5,000 crore by 2030, from around ₹1,000 crore now, per CareEdge Ratings.  

Price Correction Ahead  
- Growth to accelerate post-Semaglutide (Novo Nordisk) patent expiry, driving ~50% price drop in FY27 and another 10–30% decline in FY28 as generics enter.  

Demand Drivers  
- 60–70% of demand linked to type-2 diabetes therapy, with the rest from obesity and weight-management users.  
- Rising obesity, awareness, and improved medicine access to expand penetration to ~1% of India’s population by 2030.

- 5–6 Indian pharma players expected in the first wave of generic launches, intensifying price competition.  
- Generic manufacturers face challenges from patent disputes, complex production, and regulatory approvals.  

Key Takeaway:  
Patent expiries and rising health awareness are positioning India’s pharma sector for a multi-billion-rupee opportunity in obesity and diabetes therapeutics by 2030.

Coal Power Set to Bridge India’s Energy Gap  

Shifting Power Mix  
- With gas and hydro supply weakening, coal-based generation is poised to fill the short-term gap in electricity supply.  
- Coal currently contributes ~75% of India’s power, while renewables make up ~22%.  

Analyst View  
- Morgan Stanley expects lower gas and hydro output in H1 FY27, driving higher coal dependency.  
- In FY26, gas and hydro accounted for only ~2% and 9% of total generation, respectively.  

Fuel Dynamics  
- Rising LNG prices and potential summer hydro shortfalls may push evening power demand higher.  
- This could lift coal plant utilization and maintain pressure on supply-demand balances.  

Policy Intervention  
- The government may invoke Section 11 of the Electricity Act, 2003, directing plants to operate at full capacity during shortages.  

Investor Focus  
- Stronger demand and price-supportive conditions could benefit thermal power players like Adani Power and JSW Energy.  

Key Takeaway:  
Coal remains India’s most reliable base-load source, and potential gas and hydro deficits in FY27 are likely to bolster coal generation and thermal power valuations.

India May Absorb $3B Worth of Russian Crude Stranded at Sea  

Potential Energy Boost  
- Nearly 50 million barrels of Russian oil are stranded at sea amid shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.  
- Cargo valued at $2.3–$3 billion could find buyers in India, easing trade pressures on Moscow.  

Import Recovery Ahead  
- India’s Russian oil imports fell from ~2 million barrels/day (mid-2024) to 1.06 million barrels/day (Feb 2026).  
- Absorbing these shipments could trigger a short-term rebound in import volumes.  
- Another 25–30 million barrels remain en route, potentially adding to India’s inflows.  

Geopolitical Context  
- The Hormuz crisis threatens ~60 million tonnes of crude and ~7 million tonnes of LNG each month, straining global supply chains.  

India’s Energy Strategy  
- In Feb 2026, India imported $1.88 billion of Russian fossil fuels, with crude oil forming 81% of the total.  
- Continued focus on discounted Russian barrels underscores India’s pragmatic energy sourcing amid global volatility.  

Key Takeaway:  
India’s move to absorb stranded Russian crude reinforces its position as a strategic energy buyer, leveraging geopolitical dislocations for supply security and cost efficiency.

Oil Shocks Historically Signal S&P 500 Buying Opportunities  

Proven Track Record  
- Over 40 years, S&P 500 delivered +24% average 12-month returns after +20% 2-day oil spikes.  
- 6 out of 7 instances since 1986 saw positive 1-year gains post-shock.  

Standout Recoveries  
- Strongest rally: +54% after 2020 pandemic crash, fueled by massive stimulus.  
- Sole exception: -11% during 2008 Financial Crisis (prolonged recession).  

Pattern Insight  
- Every oil shock not tied to deep recession triggered strong subsequent rallies.  
- Shocks typically prove brief, creating long-term equity entry points.  

Key Takeaway:  
Oil price spikes remain classic dip-buying setups for S&P 500, barring systemic recession risks—history favors strategic accumulation over panic.

**HSBC Slams Brakes on Indian OMCs: Crude Squeeze Crushes Earnings**

**OMCs Face Marketing Losses**  
- Higher crude (~USD75/bbl) triggers sharp earnings cuts for IOCL, HPCL, BPCL due to mounting marketing losses.  
- Even moderate crude decks erase FY25's windfall margins (₹13-17/ltr), reverting to low-single-digit or negative profitability.  

**Downgrades Hit IOCL/HPCL/BPCL**  
- All three downgraded to **Hold** with slashed FY27-28E earnings (mid-teens to high-40% cuts vs prior).  
- Target multiples trimmed to/below 10-yr averages amid volatility and policy overhang.  
- Negative leverage: refining**HSBC Oil & Gas Alert: Crude Squeeze Hits OMCs Hard – Downgrades Galore**

**OMCs Face Marketing Losses**  
- Higher crude (~USD75/bbl+) triggers sharp earnings cuts for IOCL, HPCL, BPCL due to persistent marketing losses.  
- FY25's high margins (₹13-17/ltr) were outliers; pump price freezes now erode integrated profitability.  

**Downgrades to Hold**  
- HSBC downgrades IOCL, HPCL, BPCL to Hold: slashes FY27-28E earnings (mid-teens to high-40s% cuts).  
- Lowers target PE multiples to/below 10-yr averages amid volatility and policy overhang.  
- UBS echoes: trims targets (IOCL ₹175, BPCL ₹365, HPCL ₹340), warns $5 crude spike halves profits.  

**ONGC: Capped Upside**  
- Benefits from higher crude realizations but policy risks (govt caps ~USD75/bbl, regulated gas) limit supernormal gains.  
- Maintain Reduce: asymmetric downside from capex, project delays, subsidiary drags (HPCL/MRPL).  
- Target ~low-₹200s implies ~20% downside.  

**Positioning Shift**  
- Peak-cycle earnings over: build in lower, volatile marketing margins for OMCs.  
- Upside now valuation/technical, not fundamentals at elevated crude.  
- ONGC acts as policy-capped upstream utility, not pure crude play.  

**Key Takeaway**: De-rate OMCs, stay cautious on ONGC – crude spike flips sector from windfall to squeeze.

HSBC Slams Brakes on Indian OMCs: Crude Squeeze Crushes Earnings

OMCs Face Marketing Losses  
- Higher crude (~USD75/bbl) triggers sharp earnings cuts for IOCL, HPCL, BPCL due to mounting marketing losses.  
- Even moderate crude decks erase FY25's windfall margins (₹13-17/ltr), reverting to low-single-digit or negative profitability.  

Downgrades Hit IOCL/HPCL/BPCL  
- All three downgraded to Hold with slashed FY27-28E earnings (mid-teens to high-40% cuts vs prior).  
- Target multiples trimmed to/below 10-yr averages amid volatility and policy overhang.  
- Negative leverage: refining**HSBC Oil & Gas Alert: Crude Squeeze Hits OMCs Hard – Downgrades Galore**

OMCs Face Marketing Losses  
- Higher crude (~USD75/bbl+) triggers sharp earnings cuts for IOCL, HPCL, BPCL due to persistent marketing losses.  
- FY25's high margins (₹13-17/ltr) were outliers; pump price freezes now erode integrated profitability.  

Downgrades to Hold  
- HSBC downgrades IOCL, HPCL, BPCL to Hold: slashes FY27-28E earnings (mid-teens to high-40s% cuts).  
- Lowers target PE multiples to/below 10-yr averages amid volatility and policy overhang.  
- UBS echoes: trims targets (IOCL ₹175, BPCL ₹365, HPCL ₹340), warns $5 crude spike halves profits.  

ONGC: Capped Upside  
- Benefits from higher crude realizations but policy risks (govt caps ~USD75/bbl, regulated gas) limit supernormal gains.  
- Maintain Reduce: asymmetric downside from capex, project delays, subsidiary drags (HPCL/MRPL).  
- Target ~low-₹200s implies ~20% downside.  

Positioning Shift  
- Peak-cycle earnings over: build in lower, volatile marketing margins for OMCs.  
- Upside now valuation/technical, not fundamentals at elevated crude.  
- ONGC acts as policy-capped upstream utility, not pure crude play.  

Key Takeaway: De-rate OMCs, stay cautious on ONGC – crude spike flips sector from windfall to squeeze.

Hormuz Ship Traffic Drops to Zero Amid War Escalation

• 🚢 Shipping Halt: Vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz fell to zero for the first time since the war began.

• 🛢️ Key Energy Route: Strait normally carries ~20% of global oil & gas trade.

• 📉 Sharp Decline: Average daily crossings dropped from ~77 to just 2.7 during the war.

• ⚠️ Supply Risk: Halt raises concerns over global energy supply disruptions.

🔴 Impact: Negative
Major disruption in a critical energy route could push oil prices higher and increase global market volatility.

**JEFFERIES ON BANKS: LIMITED BUYBACKS, DIVIDEND POTENTIAL AHEAD**

**Global Buyback Boom**  
- Global banks doubled buybacks to **>$260B in 2025**, driven by higher ROE, excess capital, and slow growth.

**Indian Banks' Parallel Setup**  
- Indian banks show **rising ROE** and **slowing growth** (still above global peers).  
- Jefferies sees **limited buyback scope** despite similar tailwinds.

**Dividend Cap Hike Unlocks Value**  
- Recent **hike in dividend payout cap** enables select banks to lift shareholder returns.

**High Capital, High Payout Candidates**  
- **HDFC Bank, Union Bank, Bank of India** lead with strong capital and payout flexibility.

**Capital-Rich but Cautious Players**  
- **Kotak Bank, CSB Bank** have high capitalization but have kept payouts low.

**Key Takeaway:**  
**Prioritize dividend upside in HDFC/PSUs over buybacks — ROE strength favors payout hikes in a capped repurchase environment.**

GLOBAL ENERGY FLASH: INDIA MOVES TO STABILIZE HORMUZ CRISIS  

India–Iran Diplomacy in Focus  
- EAM S. Jaishankar confirmed India is holding direct talks with Iran to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  
- Discussions are showing early progress, aimed at easing disruptions to energy trade.  
- No blanket arrangement for Indian-flagged ships — each movement is being cleared case-by-case.  
- India prefers active coordination with Tehran over diplomatic disengagement.  

Geopolitical Tensions Remain Elevated  
- Iran’s military conflict has disrupted shipping lanes, fueling a global oil spike above $100/bbl.  
- Over 3,750 lives lost in the regional escalation so far.  
- Press TV reports Iran’s media center warning residents in parts of Dubai and Doha of impending attacks.  
- Iran claims U.S. military personnel are concealed in these locations, urging immediate evacuation.  

U.S. and Iran: Fragile Communication  
- Donald Trump signaled openness to a new deal with Iran, but on “better terms.”  
- Iran’s Abbas Araghchi denied seeking talks or a ceasefire, saying fighting continues.  
- Trump warned of potential fresh strikes on Iran’s Kharg Island oil infrastructure if hostilities persist.  

Energy CEOs Sound Alarm  
- ExxonMobil, Chevron, and ConocoPhillips CEOs cautioned the Trump administration that the energy crisis will deepen if the Strait remains blocked.  
- Global supply chains and tanker routes remain under severe stress.  

U.S.–China Trade Momentum Continues  
- U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent and Trade Representative Greer urged China to increase purchases of Boeing aircraft, U.S. coal, oil, and gas.  
- Talks included proposals for managed trade and investment frameworks ahead of the Trump–Xi Beijing Summit.  
- Technical discussions resume Monday to address rare earth access issues, including elements like yttrium vital for advanced manufacturing.  

Europe Seeks De-escalation  
- France’s Macron urged Iran’s President Pezeshkian to end attacks on neighbors like Lebanon and Iraq.  
- He stressed immediate restoration of freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.  

Trump: China Should Help Unblock Hormuz  
- Trump emphasized China’s dependence on the Strait — 90% of its oil flows through Hormuz — calling for Beijing’s active intervention.  

Key Takeaway:  
Global oil security hinges on rapid de-escalation in Hormuz — India’s diplomatic outreach may emerge as the critical stabilizing factor amid intensifying U.S.–Iran–China power play.

ADANI POWER: MSEDCL 1600 MW THERMAL LOA

Adani Power has secured a 25-year thermal power supply deal with MSEDCL for 1600 MW, starting FY30-31.

Deal Highlights  
- Tariff: ₹5.30/kWh (Yr 1).  
- 5th LT PSA FY25-26; total 10,400 MW won.  
- Cumulative LT PSAs: 13.3 GW (of 23.8 GW pipeline).  
- >95% of 18.15 GW ops capacity locked in M/LT PSAs.  
- Pre-set coal linkage for security.  
- MCap: ₹2.73 Lakh Cr.

Key Takeaway  
Pipeline execution de-risks growth; PPA stability buffers spot vol. Watch capex funding.

RAJOO ENGINEERS: CAPEX LED GROWTH CYCLE

Growth outlook

- FY26 revenue expected at ~₹350 Cr (₹300 Cr Rajoo + ~₹50 Cr Kohli from Sep).  
- Management is guiding for about 20–22% CAGR in revenue and earnings over the next few years.  
- Combined capacity (current + new 80‑acre campus) is planned for roughly 2,000 machines a year by 2030, implying ~₹1,000 Cr potential revenue if execution is on track.  

New 80‑acre campus

- Land for the new facility is registered, converted to non‑agricultural, and Bhoomi Pujan is done; Phase 1 (30 acres) is under construction.  
- Management is targeting commercial production from the new campus in about two years (around FY28), positioned as a first‑of‑its‑kind extrusion machinery hub in India for this niche.  

Solar extrusion opportunity

- Rajoo has developed an EVA/POE encapsulant film extrusion line for the solar industry, an area earlier dominated by Chinese suppliers.  
- This is India’s first such line and is expected to reach around 8–10% of revenues once adoption matures over 1–2 years, more as a strategic wedge into solar than a near‑term revenue driver.  

Demand and near‑term risks

- Global plastic extrusion machinery market is about ₹35,000 Cr equivalent and is growing with flexible packaging, agriculture and infrastructure demand.  
- In the near term, Red Sea and broader geopolitical/logistics issues are delaying order lifting and dispatches, pushing out revenue recognition rather than destroying demand.

**India’s Credit Paradox – NBFCs & MSME Credit Gap**

• **NBFCs share:** ~21% of total credit  
• **Banks share:** Majority of remaining credit

• **Core issue:** Not a capital shortage, but a **credit translation problem**

• **Credit gap:** MSMEs, microfinance & small-ticket borrowers face **₹25–30 trillion deficit**

• **Economic role:** These segments contribute **~30–40% of India’s GDP**

• **Employment:** Employ **majority of India’s non-farm workforce**

**SAMHI Hotels – PL Capital Event Update Highlights**

**Rating, Target Price & Upside**
• **Rating:** BUY maintained
• **CMP:** ₹147
• **Target Price:** ₹264 (earlier ₹290)
• Valuation based on **10.5x FY28E EBITDA** (earlier 12x)
• **Implied upside:** ~79%

**RARE India Acquisition**
• SAMHI to acquire **70% stake in RARE India** for **~₹474 Mn**
• Structure: **₹234 Mn primary + ₹240 Mn secondary investment**
• Post-deal ownership: **SAMHI 70% | Founders 30%**

• RARE portfolio: **67 hotels, ~990 rooms**
• Presence across **15+ Indian states plus Nepal & Bhutan**
• Properties include **forts, palaces, hill retreats, wildlife lodges & boutique hotels**

• Business model: **Asset-light experiential leisure platform**
• Typical **ARR ~₹25,000 per night**

**Marriott Tie-up & B2C Expansion**
• RARE to become **Marriott’s exclusive platform for “Outdoor Collection by Marriott Bonvoy”**
• Coverage across **India, Nepal, Sri Lanka & Bhutan**

• Current model: **B2B subscription**
• Fees: **₹0.2–0.4 Mn per hotel annually**
• Plus **18–20% booking commission**

• B2C stack largely **untapped so far**

• Current portfolio enterprise revenue: **~₹2,500 Mn**
• Based on **~35% occupancy & ~₹20,000 ARR**

• Expected improvements post-Marriott integration:
• **Occupancy:** ~35–45% → ~50–55%
• **ARR uplift:** ~15% over time

• Expansion pipeline:
• Currently **67 hotels**
• Active talks with **25–30 properties**
• Near-term target **~90–100 hotels**
• Medium-term target **~120–150 hotels**
• Room inventory target **~1,800–2,200 rooms**

• B2C revenue forecast:
• **FY27E:** ₹425 Mn
• **FY28E:** ₹556 Mn
• **FY29E:** ₹709 Mn

**Financial Impact**
• **EBITDA estimates raised ~4%** for FY27E & FY28E
• Driven by **new B2C commission revenue**

• Expected growth (FY26E–FY28E):
• **Revenue CAGR:** ~15%
• **EBITDA CAGR:** ~23%

**Key Financial Projections (SAMHI)**

• **Revenue**
• FY25: ₹11,205 Mn
• FY26E: ₹12,974 Mn
• FY27E: ₹14,868 Mn
• FY28E: ₹17,279 Mn

• **EBITDA**
• FY25: ₹4,071 Mn
• FY26E: ₹4,708 Mn
• FY27E: ₹5,796 Mn
• FY28E: ₹7,126 Mn

• **EBITDA Margin**
• ~36.3% (FY25–26)
• ~39.0% FY27E
• ~41.2% FY28E

• **Adjusted PAT**
• FY25: ₹872 Mn
• FY26E: ₹1,361 Mn
• FY27E: ₹2,656 Mn
• FY28E: ₹3,242 Mn

• **EPS**
• ₹3.9 → ₹6.2 → ₹12.0 → ₹14.7
• EPS growth **95.1% in FY27E**
• EPS growth **22.1% in FY28E**

**Balance Sheet Improvements**
• Total debt: **₹21,285 Mn (FY25) → ₹12,785 Mn (FY28E)**
• **Net debt/equity:** **1.8x → 0.3x**

• **RoE:** 8.0% → 12.9%
• **RoCE:** 9.1% → 14.3%

**Valuation Snapshot**
• **EV/EBITDA:** 7.2x FY27E | 5.8x FY28E
• **EV/Sales:** 2.8x FY27E | 2.4x FY28E
• **P/E:** 12.2x FY27E | 10.0x FY28E
• **P/B:** 1.4x FY27E | 1.2x FY28E

**RARE Platform Economics**
• Expected **RoCE:** ~60–70%
• Payback period: **~18–24 months**

• As network scales to **~120–125 hotels**
• Portfolio enterprise revenue potential: **~₹8,000 Mn**

• EBITDA potential:
• Current B2B model: **~₹150 Mn**
• Post B2C transition: **~₹315–400 Mn**
• Operating margin potential: **35–40%**

**Key Positives**
• Entry into **high-ARR experiential luxury segment**
• **Asset-light expansion model**
• **Marriott partnership** boosts occupancy & pricing
• Improving **balance sheet & return ratios**

**Key Watchpoints**
• Execution of **B2C platform rollout**
• **Integration with Marriott ecosystem**
• Ability to scale to **120–150 hotels**
• Demand cyclicality in **premium leisure travel**

Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders – Quick Snapshot

• CMP ~₹2,328, down ~38% from 52-week high ₹3,778
• Despite strong Q3 FY26 results, stock currently in a technical downtrend

Current Status
• Q3 FY26 Net Profit: ~₹880 Cr (↑ ~9% YoY)
• Revenue: ~₹3,601 Cr (↑ 14.5% YoY)
• EBITDA Margin: ~24.6%, indicating strong profitability
• Financial strength: Virtually debt-free with ~43.3% ROCE

Potential Tailwinds
• Order book: ₹23,700+ Cr, ensuring multi-year revenue visibility
• Strategic MoU: Trilateral partnership with Brazil for Scorpene submarine maintenance
• High indigenization: ~75% in P17A stealth frigates, reducing currency risk

Potential Headwinds
• Execution risk: Timing of submarine & destroyer deliveries may cause margin volatility
• Macro risk: Brent crude > $100 could increase logistics & input costs
• Supply chain challenges: Delays in critical marine components

Technicals & Valuation
• Stock corrected ₹3,778 → ₹2,328
• Trading in a descending channel and below 50 & 200 DMA
• Valuation still premium for PSU at ~38x P/E
• Break below support may trigger further selling pressure

Future Triggers
• Execution pace of P15B Destroyers & P17A Stealth Frigates
• Increasing share of submarine maintenance & service revenue
• Development of Next-Generation Destroyers
• Export opportunities to friendly nations

reports.text:
Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) 2.0 – Key Points & Investment Implications
• Cabinet approved JJM 2.0 extension till Dec 2028 with ₹8.69 lakh Cr total outlay
• Central assistance increased to ₹3.59 lakh Cr (earlier ₹2.08 lakh Cr)
• Mission launched in 2019 when only 17% rural households had tap water
• Coverage now reached ~81–82% of 19.36 Cr rural households
• ~3.5 Cr households still to be connected, mainly in Bihar, UP, Rajasthan & tribal regions
• Focus shifting from infrastructure creation → reliable service delivery
• Emphasis on water governance, sustainability, monitoring & quality control
• New digital framework “Sujalam Bharat” to assign unique Sujal Gaon ID to every village
• System will digitally map water supply chain from source to tap
• Jal Arpan mechanism where Gram Panchayats certify completion & O&M readiness
• Annual “Jal Utsav” for community-led maintenance & monitoring
• Programme aligned with Viksit Bharat @2047 vision aiming 24×7 rural drinking water supply

Funding Structure & Risks
• Entire ₹8.69 lakh Cr will not come from the Centre
• Funding structure: 100% for UTs, 90% for Himalayan & North-East states, 50% for other states
• Central funds will be milestone-based under JJM 2.0 guidelines
• States must meet structural reforms like digital asset mapping
• If states fail to provide 50% share or meet criteria, central funds may stop
• Maharashtra & West Bengal earlier saw fund pauses due to funding gaps or complaints

Governance & Transparency
• Full digital mapping of water infrastructure
• Stronger monitoring, compliance & audit systems
• Designed to avoid payment delays seen under JJM 1.0

Corruption / Implementation Issues
• Kerala CAG report (Feb 2026): 2.14 lakh connections disconnected within 1 month
• Suggests possible ghost or non-functional infrastructure
• Uttar Pradesh recorded highest complaints: 14,264 cases investigated

Sector Impact
• JJM 1.0 winners: Pipe manufacturers & EPC players
• JJM 2.0 focus sectors: Pumps, valves, flow control, water treatment & EPC
• Potential beneficiaries: Shakti Pumps, Oswal Pumps, Kirloskar Brothers, VA Tech Wabag, Ion Exchange, SPML Infra, Astral, Supreme Industries, Jash Engineering

Investment Takeaway
• Geo-spatial mapping companies & water infra players could benefit
• However execution risk, political risk & state funding dependency remain
• Proper due diligence required before investing in JJM-linked stocks

Jal Jeevan 2.0 approved with outlay of 8.7lk cr

JAL JEEVAN MISSION 2.0
JAL JEEVAN MISSION 2.0

Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM 2.0) – Cabinet Approval & Key Highlights

Mission Extension & Budget
• Cabinet approved extension of Jal Jeevan Mission till Dec 2028
• Total outlay increased to ₹8.69 lakh crore
• Central assistance raised to ₹3.59 lakh crore (from ₹2.08 lakh crore)
• Additional central share: ~₹1.51 lakh crore

Policy Shift
• Focus shifting from infrastructure creation → service delivery model
• Emphasis on water governance, sustainability & institutional framework
• Aim: Reliable rural piped drinking water supply

Digital & Governance Framework
• Launch of “Sujalam Bharat” digital platform
• Each village assigned a unique Sujal Gaon / Service Area ID
• Entire water supply chain mapped digitally (source → tap)

• Jal Arpan system: Gram Panchayats & VWSCs to participate in scheme commissioning
• GP certification mandatory before declaring “Har Ghar Jal”
• Ensures operation & maintenance systems are in place

• Annual “Jal Utsav” community event to review and maintain village water systems

Current Progress
• 2019 baseline: 3.23 crore households (17%) had tap water
• 12.56 crore new connections added under JJM
• Current coverage: ~15.80 crore rural households
• Tap water coverage: ~81.61% of 19.36 crore rural households

Socio-Economic Impact
• 9 crore women freed from daily water collection (SBI Research)
• 5.5 crore hours/day saved in women’s drudgery (WHO estimate)
• Potential 4 lakh diarrheal deaths prevented
• 14 million DALYs saved

• Estimated 30% reduction in under-five mortality (~1.36 lakh lives annually)
• Employment impact: 59.9 lakh direct + 2.2 crore indirect person-years

Future Targets
• Tap water for all 19.36 crore rural households by Dec 2028
• All Gram Panchayats to be certified “Har Ghar Jal”
• Move towards 24×7 rural drinking water supply

Strategic Vision
• Supports Viksit Bharat @2047
• Convergence across multiple ministries for long-term water sustainability
• Shift to citizen-centric utility model for rural water services

Airfloa – Business Overview & Growth Opportunities

Business Model Evolution
• Started as a component supplier (frames, diffusers)
• Moved to assembly supplier (seat systems, floor systems, toilet modules)
• Now a turnkey system integrator offering design, supply, installation & commissioning

• Currently a Tier-1 supplier to ICF, MCF, RCF, Alstom & Siemens
• Turnkey contracts create high entry barriers due to engineering capability, execution expertise & integrated supply chain

Diversification – Defence & Aerospace
• Received ₹600+ Mn orders from HAL for AMCA simulator systems
• Simulator demand typically 5–10× aircraft production due to pilot training needs
• Working on autonomous drones & anti-drone systems
• Also developing laser-based air defence technologies with Israeli technology partners

Emerging Opportunities
• Platform Screen Doors (PSD) in metro projects → ~₹20,000 Mn annual opportunity
• Plans to participate via partnership with BEL

• Indian Railways coach refurbishment program: ~₹2,60,000 Mn opportunity
• Company aims to participate as a turnkey integrator

• Exploring joint ventures with PSU partners for railway coach maintenance
• Potential to create recurring revenue streams

▪️ Renewables + infra ecosystem gaining traction (Waaree, BHEL, Adani Green)
▪️ Real estate & financials seeing selective profit booking
▪️ Consumption/export smallcaps like Avanti Feeds & Kansai Nerolac seeing accumulation
▪️ Trend suggests sector rotation rather than risk-off

Source: ICICI Direct Research  
Note: No Buy/Sell Recommendation

India’s Growing Repair Economy

• ~20 crore smartphones sold in India every year
• Yet local repair shops fixing screens under ₹500 remain common across cities
• This has created a ~₹15,000 Cr smartphone repair economy, growing steadily
• India’s electronic equipment repair market: ~$8.2 Bn (~₹68,000 Cr) in 2024
• Expected to reach ~$14 Bn (~₹1.17 Lakh Cr) by 2033
• 90%+ repair revenue comes from out-of-warranty devices
• Reason: Repairing is cheaper than replacing
• Devices often get repaired, resold & reused for another 2–3 years
• Unlike the West where broken devices often end up in drawers or landfills
• Government launched the Right to Repair portal supporting this ecosystem
• A practical habit that has now become a large economic ecosystem

Shree Refrigerations – Key Updates

New Factory & Capacity Expansion
• 50,000 sq ft Phase-1 facility under construction on 6-acre land, ~15 km from existing plant near Karad–Pune highway
• Machinery trials: April | Full operations: Q1 FY27
• Phase-2: Additional 50,000 sq ft expansion planned
• Target capacity: ~450–500 kilo tonnes engineering gravity
• Key objective: Reduce subcontracting (painting, fabrication, machining) to improve delivery & margins
• Facility to include robotic welding, paint shop & laser cutting

New Business – Data Centers
• Agreement signed with SMARD (global data center cooling player)
• Role: Service, installation & commissioning support in India
• Technology: Oil-free magnetic bearing compressors → ~40% power savings
• Strategy: Import equipment initially; manufacturing later once volumes scale
• New opportunities: Edge/modular data centers & BESS cooling
• First BESS chiller order already received

Other Highlights
• Working capital heavy business — ~85% capital in current assets
• H1 FY26 PAT dip: Due to new project onboarding costs
• Revenue recognition back-ended, H2 expected to normalize
• ROE/ROCE diluted post IPO fund raise, improvement expected with growth
• In-house facility to solve painting & machining supply delays
Strategic mapping of top transformer players in India

Shipbuilding Sector – Key Notes

• Geopolitical importance: Shipbuilding is a strategic sector driven by strong government focus, political will, and expansion of blue-water navies (including aircraft carriers) to maintain global dominance.

• Government support critical: Financing cost is a key challenge — shipbuilding loans cost ~4% in China vs ~10% in India, creating a major competitiveness gap.

• Global market share: Shipbuilding is dominated by China (~55%), South Korea (~30%), and Japan (~10%). Around 8 lakh people are employed in this industry globally. Japan faces ageing workforce issues, while Korean companies are exploring shipyard development in India.

• Skilled labour requirement: Industry requires specialized workers including welding, piping, and other marine engineering skills.

• Technology partnerships: Samsung Heavy Industries has partnered with Indian players to transfer know-how and enhance shipbuilding capabilities.

• India’s shipping dependency: About 96% of vessels used by India are foreign-owned, making shipping the second-largest forex outflow after crude oil imports.

• Cochin Shipyard: One of India’s key shipbuilders with large ship construction and maintenance capabilities. Shipyards essentially act as factories for ship production and repair.

• Shipyard classification: Riverine shipyards operate in smaller waterways and handle smaller displacement vessels, while large ocean-based shipyards build bigger ships.

• Capex interpretation: Sector capex is typically visible through number of ships produced or expansion of shipyard capacity.

• Defence opportunity: Indian Navy allocation could increase from ~18% to ~40% of total defence outlay, with ~70 new ships in pipeline, creating large incremental order opportunities over the next decade.

• Shipping ecosystem revival: Government pushing for domestic shipping with contracts involving Shipping Corporation of India, HPCL, and BPCL.

• Dredging importance: Dredging Corporation of India received ₹300 Cr government infusion. Dredging removes sediment for port development and inland waterways expansion.

• GE Shipping business model: Primarily a ship ownership and rental (charter) model, evaluated via NAV approach and cash flow generation, though earnings depend on volatile global charter rates.

• Dredging growth focus: Government intends to significantly expand dredging capacity as part of port and waterway infrastructure development.

• Long order cycles: Shipbuilding projects have long timelines — submarines (~12 years) and patrol vessels (~3 years). Investors must analyze orderbook duration and stage-wise execution.

• Key raw material: Marine-grade steel supplied by companies such as SAIL and JSW Steel.

• Propulsion systems: Account for 25–30% of ship cost (around 30% for warships). Naval surface guns have been partly indigenized with Israeli technology support (cost ₹20–30 Cr per gun). GRSE has received large orders.

• Component suppliers: Kirloskar (diesel engines), Elecon and Triveni (power transmission gears).

• Key industry KPIs: Orderbook visibility (years), timeline mapping of projects, global capability comparison, and L1 bidder positioning.

India’s Credit Paradox – NBFCs & MSME Credit Gap

• NBFCs share: ~21% of total credit  
• Banks share: Majority of remaining credit

• Core issue: Not a capital shortage, but a credit translation problem

• Credit gap: MSMEs, microfinance & small-ticket borrowers face ₹25–30 trillion deficit

• Economic role: These segments contribute ~30–40% of India’s GDP

• Employment: Employ majority of India’s non-farm workforce

📊 **SECTOR ANALYSIS INDIA EV LANDSCAPE**

🚘 **India EV Landscape – Key Structural Points**

**EV Market Structure**
• EV ecosystem: **E-2W, E-3W, E-PV**
• Growth drivers: **battery cost decline, OEM participation, charging infra**
• Subsidies tapering (**FAME II / PM e-Drive**)
• **FY26 turning point:** legacy OEMs gaining share, startups losing share

➡️ **Electric Two-Wheelers (E-2W)**

**Industry**
• FY26 volume: **~1.3M units**
• Growth: **~15% YoY**

**Key Driver**
• EV running cost: **₹0.15–0.25/km**
• Petrol scooter: **~₹2/km**

**Policy**
• GST cut on ICE <350cc to **18%**
• EV-ICE price gap narrowed

**Market Share (Feb-26)**
• **TVS:** ~28%  
• **Bajaj:** ~23%  
• **Ather:** ~18%  
• **Hero:** ~11%  
• **Top 4 share:** ~80%

**Structural Trend**
• Shift from **startup disruption → legacy OEM consolidation**

👉 **Company Highlights – E-2W**

**TVS Motor**
• Market leader
• iQube demand strong
• Avg sales: **~25k/month**
• Peak: **~34.8k units**
• FY26 EV growth: **~33%**

**Bajaj Auto**
• Rare earth magnet shortage impact
• Production drop: **~12k units**
• Recovery: **~19.5k units**
• Focus: **sub-₹1L commuter EV scooters**

**Ather Energy**
• Pivot: **premium → family segment**
• **Rizta:** ~70% sales
• Capacity: **0.42M → 1.42M units**

**Ola Electric**
• Sales collapse:
• **53k/month → ~4k**
• Issues: service, supply chain, delivery delays

**Hero MotoCorp**
• VIDA platform expansion
• Sales: **6.5k → 12.5k**
• **Battery-as-a-Service (BaaS)** model

👉 **Electric Passenger Vehicles (E-PV)**

**Industry**
• FY26 volume: **~200k**
• Growth: **~84% YoY**

**Market Share**
• **Tata Motors:** ~40%
• **MG Motor:** ~25-30%
• **Mahindra:** ~15%
• **Top 3 share:** ~85%

👉 **Company Highlights – E-PV**

**Tata Motors**
• Models across **value → premium segments**
• Key launches:
• **Harrier EV**
• **Curvv EV**
• **Sierra EV**

**Mahindra Electric**
• Models: **BE6, XEV 9e**
• Sales peak: **~4.2k/month**
• Capacity target: **7–8k/month FY27**

**JSW MG Motor**
• **Windsor EV:** >75% EV sales
• Demand cooling in **2H FY26**
• New model: **MG 520 PHEV (2026)**

👉 **Electric Three-Wheelers (E-3W)**

**Industry**
• Fastest EV adoption segment
• Growth: **~16% YTD**

**Market Structure**
• Regional players share: **>55%**

**Policy**
• **Subsidies ended Dec-2025**

👉 **Company Highlights – E-3W**

**Mahindra**
• Platforms: **Treo, e-Alfa**
• New model: **UDO Autoplane**
• Range: **~265 km**

**Bajaj Auto**
• Models: **GoGo, Riki**
• New EV: **WEGO P9018**
• Range: **~296 km**

**YC Electric**
• Sales decline: **3.4k → 1.3k**
• Impact: subsidy removal + Bajaj competition

👉Core Structural Themes
• **Legacy OEM dominance increasing**
• **E-3W electrifying fastest**
• **EV passenger cars entering early mass adoption**
• **Subsidy dependence declining**
• EV adoption now driven by **Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)**

✏️**Investment Takeaways**
• **Legacy OEMs biggest EV winners**
• **EV startups face scale challenges**
• **E-3W strongest electrification**
• **Passenger EV market entering growth phase**
• EV shift: **subsidy driven → economics driven**

reports.text:
**INDIA EV AUTO: LEGACY OEMs DOMINATE UPTREND**

**Big Picture**  
EV upcycle intact across E-2W/3W/PV, but **legacy OEMs consolidating** (~80% share) as startups falter on service/capital/execution. FY26 volumes: E-2W >1.3 Mn (+15%), E-PV ~200k (+84%), E-3W +16% YTD.

**E-2W Leadership**  
TVS **28%**, Bajaj **23%**, Ather **18%**, Hero **11%** = **80% share**.  
TVS iQube peaks ~35k/mo; Bajaj Chetak C2501 <₹1L; Ather Rizta family pivot; Hero VIDA BaaS ₹45k + ₹0.96/km.  
**Ola collapse**: ~53k → ~4k/mo on service woes.

**E-PV Concentration**  
Tata **40%**, JSW MG **25-30%**, Mahindra **15%** = **85%**.  
Tata full-stack (Tiago to Harrier.ev, BaaS Punch); Mahindra BE6/XEV 9e scaling to 7-8k pm FY27; JSW Windsor >75%, MG 520 PHEV Oct'26.  
Maruti e-Vitara entry; Hyundai/BYD ~3-4%.

**E-3W Acceleration**  
Fastest growth; fleet-driven (passenger/e-comm).  
Bajaj overtakes Mahindra; TVS +1400% YTD low-base. Subsidy end Dec'25 but momentum holds.

**Company Spotlights**  
**TVS**: iQube + Orbiter; ~33% EV growth vs 15% industry.  
**Bajaj**: Chetak/GoGo; 2W/3W swapping.  
**Mahindra**: Treo/e-Alfa ~12% 3W EV share FY26; XEV 9S deliveries.  
**Tata Motors**: Punch/Harrier/Curvv ramps.

**Themes**  
Service networks/reliability win; TCO > subsidies; GST ICE cuts narrow gap but EVs hold on running costs (~₹0.2/km vs ₹2 petrol). BaaS widens access.

**Key Takeaway**  
Legacy scale (TVS/Bajaj/Tata/Mahindra) trumps startups—position for consolidation phase.

---

---

📍 LEADERSHIP CHANGES

GREAVES COTTON: CFO Akhila Balachandar resigns effective 19 March 2026; Manish Poddar appointed Group CFO.

---

📍 NEW PROJECTS

RAMKY INFRA: Signs ₹3,000 Cr concession agreement with MIDC to develop Maha Integrated Life Sciences City in Raigad with 95-year concession.

---

BRITANNIA: Clarifies industrial gas supply concerns will not disrupt manufacturing operations; confirms adequate finished goods inventory.

**LNG vs LPG Shortage Impact: Industry Breakdown**

**Core Differences**  
**LNG** (Liquefied Natural Gas): Methane-based, cryogenic (-162°C), used for power generation, industry, city gas.  
**LPG** (Liquefied Petroleum Gas): Refinery byproduct (propane/butane), cylinder-based, used for cooking, heating, autos.

**LNG Consumption Split (India)**  
- **Power**: ~45% (45 MMSCMD) – Gas plants face load cuts.  
- **Fertilizer**: ~25% (25 MMSCMD) – Urea production hit.  
- **City Gas (CGD)**: ~15% (15 MMSCMD) – CNG autos, PNG homes.  
- **Refineries/Petrochem**: ~10% (10 MMSCMD) – Feedstock squeeze.  
- **Others (Steel/Cement)**: ~5% (5 MMSCMD) – Industrial kilns.

**Industry Impacts**  
**Power**: Forced coal shift (+ve Coal India/NTPC); spot tariffs spike.  
**Fertilizer**: Subsidy burden up; urea imports surge.  
**CGD (IGL/MGL)**: CNG price hikes; PNG residential cutbacks.  
**Ceramics/Glass**: Kiln shutdowns (Somany hit earlier).  
**Hotels/Catering**: LPG pivot strained (just reported shortages).

**LPG Shortage Preview**  
Refinery cuts → cylinder queues → rural distress → organized players gain.

**Key Takeaway**  
LNG crunch hits power/fertilizer hardest (70% usage); LPG pinch squeezes hospitality/rural — coal/power stocks resilient amid energy pivot.

**CLSA: IRAN CONFLICT RISKS INDIA'S ENERGY SUPPLY**  

**Vulnerability Hierarchy**  
- **LPG most vulnerable**, followed by **LNG**.  
- Alternate supply support arrives only **end-April**.  
- **Acute shortages** likely over **next 3–4 weeks**.  

**Sector Output Impacts**  
- **Fertilisers, petrochemicals**: Production disruptions expected.  
- **Restaurants, glass, ceramics**: Significant capacity hits.  
- **Petrol & diesel**: Lower risk as India remains **net exporter**.  

**Corporate Pressure Points**  
- **OMCs**: IOC, BPCL, HPCL face **margin compression**.  
- **Gas utilities**: GAIL, IGL, MGL, Guj Gas, Petronet LNG under strain.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
LPG/LNG supply crunch threatens key industries short-term — OMCs/gas utilities vulnerable, but petrol/diesel exports provide defensive buffer.

**Portfolio Changes**  
- **Removed:** HPCL and BHEL.  
- **Increased Allocation:** **InterGlobe Aviation**, **Larsen & Toubro**, and **Thermax** — by **1 ppt each**.  

**Market View**  
- UBS maintains an **“Attractive” rating** on Indian equities.  
- Sees **robust macro fundamentals** and **proactive policy support** sustaining growth momentum.  

**Policy & Growth Drivers**  
- Government has **front-loaded growth measures** — including **income and GST tax cuts**, along with **labor law simplification**.  
- **RBI** has **cut rates** and **injected liquidity**, ensuring policy coordination to support expansion.  
- These steps are expected to **keep GDP growth near trend**, with **FY26 growth seen at 7.4%**.  

**Earnings Outlook**  
- **Nifty EPS growth:** projected at **single-digit** for **FY26**, rising to **double-digit** in **FY27–FY28**.  
- Medium-term view remains **constructive**, led by **ongoing reforms**, **resilient domestic demand**, and **steady corporate earnings growth**.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
UBS sees India as a structural outperformer in Asia — policy-led growth support, reform momentum, and earnings recovery underpin its positive outlook.

**Growth Outlook**  
- Nomura expects all four NBFCs to post **high-teens to 20%+ loan growth CAGR** over **FY26–28**.  
- **Three of the four** to see **ROE expansion beyond mid-teens** by FY28 as profitability strengthens.  
- Sector theme: **"Diversified lenders – Everyone, everywhere, all the time."**  

**Stock-Specific Views**  
- **Tata Capital** – Strong loan growth trajectory with ample headroom for **profitability expansion**.  
- **Piramal Finance & L&T Finance** – Undergoing **structural transformations**; both seen as **long-term compounders** with meaningful **upside potential**.  
- **HDB Finance** – Needs **rebound in loan growth** momentum for a **re-rating catalyst**.  

**Sector Insight**  
- Nomura highlights Indian regulators’ **preparation for AI adoption** across lending ecosystems, supporting digital scalability and prudent risk management.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Nomura sees India’s leading diversified NBFCs entering a new growth cycle – led by digital transformation, improving ROEs, and strong balance-sheet discipline.
